title: Feasibility, Alternatives, Risk Experiments, and Stop Decisions · Claim Audit: Feasibility Budget
summary: A long capstone plan assumes access to data, users, infrastructure, expertise, approvals, integrations, time, and operating budget that have not been tested. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the capstone feasibility budget increment in feasibility proof and alternative decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debuggi…
objectives: Estimate development, research, evaluation, accessibility, security, operations, support, contingency, and retirement effort with uncertainty.
Design the cheapest ethical experiment that distinguishes outcomes for the highest uncertainty and consequence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Feasibility Budget · Theory Feasibility Budget

theory case — The plan assumes unavailable data, participants, approval, services, expertise, operating budget, and time while rejecting no-build alternatives. content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence. Apply that move specifically to Feasibility Budget while producing feasibility proof and alternative decision record.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: The plan assumes unavailable data, participants, approval, services, expertise, operating budget, and time while rejecting no-build alternatives. The worked example variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Estimate development, research, evaluation, accessibility, security, operations, support, contingency, and retirement effort with uncertainty. A defensible solution applies Feasibility Budget, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to feasibility proof and alternative decision record.

content.code:
# Worked focus: capstone-feasibility-budget — Estimate development, research, evaluation, accessibility, security, operations, support, contingency, and retirement effort with uncertainty.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: changed-product-5
focus: capstone-feasibility-budget
decision-evidence: Estimate development, research, evaluation, accessibility, security, operations, support, contingency, and retirement effort with uncertainty.
stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

stimulus.title: Feasibility Budget failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Implementation estimates cover the full cost of a product.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Estimate development, research, evaluation, accessibility, security, operations, support, contingency, and retirement effort with uncertainty.

options.text: For repair the feasibility budget defect, apply Feasibility Budget without its stated preconditions, omit the feasibility proof and alternative decision record trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. Reconstruct the conditions for Feasibility Budget, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.

Keep the Feasibility Budget artifact unchanged and rewrite its explanation so the unsupported output appears intentional. That claim fails the repair the feasibility budget defect evidence boundary for Feasibility Budget.

steps.title: Defend the Feasibility Budget model · Theory Feasibility Budget
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Feasibility Budget: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — The plan assumes unavailable data, participants, approval, services, expertise, operating budget, and time while rejecting no-build alternatives. The theory variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. content.title: Evidence defense
